title: Scaligeria kuramensis
taxonomy: Scaligeria kuramensis is a species within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is a type of flowering plant native to Pakistan and the Himalayan region. This plant is classified as a perennial herb and belongs to the genus Scaligeria. Scaligeria kuramensis is further classified as a member of the order Apiales. The classification of Scaligeria kuramensis is supported by its characteristics and phylogenetic analysis.

intro: Scaligeria kuramensis, commonly known as Kurram scaligeria, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the surrounding areas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Scaligeria kuramensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, with the most common being fever-reducing and digestive aid formulations.
